content:
A friend of mine has been building something over the past few months.
It started with one question: Why is there no public data on everyday corruption in India?
We've all heard stories someone being asked to pay at the RTO, a police station, or while accessing another public service. But stories stay stories. They don't help us understand patterns.
He built bribes.fyi a privacy-first platform where anyone can anonymously report bribery incidents, explore trends by state and department, and generate a formal complaint letter to the appropriate authority. According to him, reports are fully anonymous, including IP addresses.
https://bribes.fyi/
It's still early, so the dataset is small. If you've ever paid or refused a bribe while accessing a public service, it'd be great if you could take a minute to submit an anonymous report.
I'm posting this because his Reddit account doesn't have enough karma yet. He'll be replying to comments himself, so if you have feedback, questions, or suggestions about the idea, please leave them below.
https://preview.redd.it/znx18evq98bh1.png?width=2936&format=png&auto=webp&s=2f9e4ef716b2797c245ecb7ae0d335e095423138
https://preview.redd.it/mk5bz9nu98bh1.png?width=2936&format=png&auto=webp&s=1a025ad10b93376c4ca1ecf13b375d5249f02564
